expectations.text: Answers use the review format with clear Recommendation, Why, and Trade-offs sections
expectations.evidence: All 3 answers use bold 'Recommendation:', 'Why:', and 'Trade-offs:' headers consistently.

expectations.text: Answers reference the existing Knowlune tech stack (React, Vite, Tailwind, or specific patterns found in codebase)
expectations.evidence: References Vite + React 19 SPA, createBrowserRouter, 22 zustand stores, Dexie, useLiveQuery, PWA/ServiceWorker, vite-plugin-pwa, specific files (Reports.tsx, vite.config.ts).

expectations.text: Explicitly indicates waiting for user approval before proceeding (review mode behavior)
expectations.evidence: The output is formatted as a review document but does not explicitly say 'waiting for your approval' or similar. It presents the answers but doesn't pause for confirmation.

expectations.text: Answers demonstrate technical depth — not surface-level generic advice
expectations.evidence: Mentions specific store counts (22), specific caching strategies in the codebase (CacheFirst, StaleWhileRevalidate), Web Workers for heavy computation, lru-cache dependency.
